last_substantive_baseline.title: Add read-only publication predeployment readiness
last_substantive_baseline.claim_boundary: This baseline proves PR #45 was merged and the read-only readiness workflow is present in the repository. It does not prove readiness dispatch, demo backend deployment, API deployment, listener verification, frontend API use, report publication, or collector replacement.

repository_observation.head_semantics: PR #45 merge commit observed before opening scoped demo-backend/API PR #46
repository_observation.claim_boundary: This is a time-bounded repository observation. Resolve live main, pull requests, CI, and Azure state whenever this file is read.

authored_change.write_owner: This ChatGPT conversation — bounded repository implementation authorized by Anthony Edgar with 'Proceed' after correcting scope to demo backends, a service listener, and a frontend-connected API
authored_change.scope: Add a collector-independent Bicep entrypoint for the two synthetic VPN backends and a public Azure Function API, package the Python API, connect the GitHub Pages Run incident analysis action to real correlated Azure transactions with controlled fallback, add scoped What-If/deploy/verify automation, tests, documentation, and current project state. No workflow is dispatched and no Azure mutation is performed by this repository increment.
verification_criteria: the scoped Bicep entrypoint references the existing VNet, edge subnet, load balancer, public IP, and Log Analytics workspace without declaring the collector
the two existing synthetic backend contracts remain VPN-01 healthy and VPN-02 radius-timeout on HTTPS 443
the Function API exposes GET /api/health and POST /api/demo/run and does not accept a caller-controlled target URL
attempt counts are bounded to 2 through 50 and expected backend HTTP 503 bodies are preserved as transaction evidence
the API response preserves exact_root_cause_claimed false and technician-owned diagnosis
the frontend invokes the live API from the Run incident analysis button and clearly falls back to the committed fixture when unavailable
the deployment workflow is exact-commit bound, excludes resolve_vm_plan.sh and the collector, and requires exact typed confirmation
What-If rejects Modify, Delete, Replace, unexpected creates, and changes to shared network, observability, or collector resources
project validation, all infrastructure tests, main Bicep, and scoped Bicep lint/build pass on the exact PR head
the final diff remains limited to the declared paths
authored_change.failure_behavior: Keep PR #46 draft, inspect the exact test or Bicep failure, patch only declared paths, and do not dispatch the workflow or mutate Azure.
authored_change.rollback: Close PR #46 without merge or revert its repository commits. No Azure cleanup applies because this increment does not dispatch or deploy.

bounded_authority_grants.authorization_source: Explicit user instruction after the read-only Azure planning boundary was stated
bounded_authority_grants.claim_boundary: This grant authorizes Azure reads and ProviderNoRbac planning only. It does not authorize PR #46's demo-backend/API workflow, deployment, collector changes, RBAC changes, report publication, guest commands, or any Azure mutation.

completed_increments.title: Decouple live report publication from collector compute
Repair read-only planner RBAC evidence capture
Use ProviderNoRbac for read-only ARM planning
Use Blob endpoint for browser report CORS
chore: reconcile merged publication project state
Repin live publication to planner run 29979955391

latest_scope_observation.interpretation: The run targeted the full collector-owning lifecycle scope rather than the demo-backend/API scope. It performed Azure login and reads but no Bicep deployment, backend deployment, API deployment, report deployment, or collector replacement.

proven: exact reviewed-commit checkout and bounded read-only authority validation succeeded
Azure OIDC login and current resource-group and collector inventory succeeded
no tagged report Storage account or visible collector publication role existed
What-If contained 21 Ignore and exactly four Create changes
the creates were one Storage account, one Blob service, one $web Blob-only container, and one Storage-scoped collector role assignment
zero Modify, Delete, and Replace changes were present
GitHub artifact digest and all 13 internal evidence-file hashes verified
no Azure mutation was authorized or performed
unproven: current Storage pricing, quota, and policy allowance
current collector identity and publisher availability at execution time
browser rendering and committed live frontend source

latest_promoted_evidence.freshness_boundary: This evidence applies to the separate public-report publication architecture and does not establish current demo-backend/API state. Fresh scoped Azure inventory and What-If are required before any demo deployment.

pr41_publication_execution_promotion.claim_boundary: Repository promotion is merged. No publication workflow execution, Azure deployment, RBAC mutation, managed-identity publication, Blob/browser proof, or manual repair is established.

pr44_publication_planner_repin.claim_boundary: Current planner evidence is machine-pinned. No publication workflow dispatch, Azure authentication for execution, permission grant, deployment, or browser proof is established.

pr45_publication_predeployment_readiness.claim_boundary: The read-only readiness workflow is present on main. It has not been dispatched and establishes no current readiness evidence or Azure authority.

pr46_demo_backends_api.claim_boundary: Repository implementation only. No scoped What-If, deployment, backend listener verification, API verification, frontend live rendering, or Azure mutation is established.

authority_defaults.rule: Repository implementation authority does not authorize workflow dispatch, Azure authentication, backend or API deployment, collector changes, or any other Azure mutation. The separate historical publication-planner grant remains the only active bounded Azure-read exception.

live_state_resolution.repository_head: Query the live GitHub default branch at read time.
live_state_resolution.pull_requests: Query live PR head, draft state, mergeability, reviews, threads, and exact-head CI.
live_state_resolution.ci: Query workflow runs and jobs for the exact commit under evaluation.
live_state_resolution.azure: Use fresh scoped Azure inventory and What-If; repository declarations and the failed broad lifecycle run are insufficient.
